source.title: Learning the value of information in an uncertain world

confidence_rationale: Direct empirical and computational evidence that anterior cingulate cortex computes a system-level volatility estimate that modulates learning rate. The single-region locus is exactly what Q-041 calls a meta-level threshold supervisor; cross-subject variation in ACC signal predicts behavioural learning-rate variation, which is the strongest possible mapping. Some discount because the task probes one decision domain (binary reward/no-reward), not the cross-substrate coordination Q-041 specifically targets.
failure_signatures: If REE produces coherent meta-stability without a unified locus, the Behrens result implies REE is missing a candidate biological mechanism that the brain does have.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Human subjects estimate environmental volatility in a way that approaches the optimal Bayesian observer. The volatility estimate modulates trial-by-trial learning rate. fMRI BOLD in anterior cingulate cortex tracks this estimate; cross-subject variation in ACC signal predicts cross-subject variation in learning rate.
mapping.ree_translation: ACC behaves as the kind of meta-level threshold supervisor Q-041 describes -- a single locus that aggregates outcome statistics across time, computes a system-level uncertainty quantity, and exports it to downstream gain controls. This is concrete evidence in favour of the unified-supervisor reading of Q-041 (one node is doing the cross-aggregation work) rather than the scattered-loci reading. The closest REE analogue currently is ARC-016's per-step EMA, but that runs at a single timescale (~10 steps), is local to E3, and does not aggregate across SD-032c/d/e or MECH-258. Behrens specifically shows that the brain uses a separate, slower, integrative signal whose timescale is set by the volatility regime itself.
mapping.mapping_caveat: ACC volatility tracking has been observed in a single decision domain (probabilistic reward learning). The paper does not establish that the same ACC signal coordinates thresholds in motor commit, sleep recalibration, or interoceptive setpoint drift -- those are the three specific gaps Q-041 raises. The 'supervisor' reading is therefore an extrapolation beyond what the paper directly measures.
mapping.source_context: Healthy human adults; probabilistic reward-learning fMRI task with periodic volatility shifts; 3T BOLD; Bayesian model comparison.

confidence_components.notes: High source quality (Nature Neuroscience, replicated across 1000+ citations of follow-on volatility-tracking work). Mapping fidelity moderate because the paper isolates volatility tracking but does not test the cross-substrate aggregation Q-041 targets. Transfer risk moderate -- the human cortex has structures REE does not yet model at this granularity.
